subject: Arts

sections.heading: 🎭 Your Quest Begins — What is Theatre?
sections.body: Theatre is like a magical realm where stories, feelings, and imagination come alive! It is not just acting on a stage — it teaches you real wizard-like powers such as speaking in front of others, working with a team, and understanding how people feel. Just like how cricket needs a whole team to win, theatre needs everyone working together. In this quest, there are no wrong answers — only brave explorers!
keyPoints: Theatre builds real-life powers like public speaking, teamwork, and empathy
It is a safe space — your ideas are always respected, never laughed at
Everyone learns differently, so you can express yourself in your own unique way

sections.heading: 😮 The Hot Seat Spell — Discovering Emotions
sections.body: Imagine your teacher is reading a story — you close your eyes, picture every detail, and suddenly she says 'LOOK!' — and you react with your face, voice, and body! This is the Hot Seat game, and it helps you discover emotions you feel every day. Think about this: your team wins a cricket match but your favourite player got out for zero. You feel happy AND sad at the same time! That is totally normal — emotions can mix together like colours in Holi.
keyPoints: Emotions can come in combinations — like feeling excited and nervous before a school play
Your face, voice, and body all work together to show what you feel inside
Recognising and naming your emotions is a true superpower

sections.heading: ✨ The Nine-Rasa Dragon — Navarasa
sections.body: Ancient wise rishis of India spent years studying human emotions and discovered something magical — all emotions in the world can be sorted into just NINE groups called Navarasa! These are like nine dragons, each with its own power: Love, Humour, Heroism, Fear, Sadness, Disgust, Anger, Wonder, and Peace. Think of your favourite Bollywood movie — every scene uses one of these nine rasas! The rasa you feel depends on your bhava, which is your own attitude or way of thinking. That is why you laugh at a joke your friend finds boring!
keyPoints: The nine rasas are: Sringara (love), Hasya (humour), Veera (heroic), Bhayanaka (fear), Karuna (sadness), Bhibhatsa (disgust), Raudra (anger), Adbhuta (wonder), Shanta (peace)
Bhava is your inner attitude — it decides which rasa you experience in a situation
Two people in the same situation can feel different emotions because their bhava is different

sections.heading: 📖 The Ancient Wizard's Spell Book — Natyashastra
sections.body: Long ago, Brahma noticed the Devas had become lazy and bored — so he created a special book called Natya Veda by taking the best wisdom from all four Vedas. But the Devas could not understand it! So Brahma called a wise rishi named Bharata Muni, who rewrote it in a simpler way — this became the Natyashastra. This incredible book is over 2000 years old, has 36 chapters and thousands of verses, and covers everything about performing arts — from costumes and music to emotions and stage lights. It is so powerful it is called the Fifth Veda!
keyPoints: Natyashastra was written by Bharata Muni and is one of the oldest books on performing arts in the world
It was created by combining wisdom from the Rigveda, Yajurveda, Samaveda, and Atharvaveda
It covers everything in theatre — emotions, music, dance, costume, makeup, and even the responsibility of artists towards their audience

sections.heading: 🏆 Mastery Unlocked — Theatre Skills for Life
sections.body: Theatre is not just about becoming an actor or a director — it gives you mastery over real life! When you perform on stage, you learn how to handle nervousness, just like a batsman facing the first ball of a match. Circle Time at the end of every class is like a team huddle in cricket — everyone sits together, shares thoughts freely, and no one is judged. These small moments build big courage and help you grow into a confident, kind, and creative person.
keyPoints: Circle Time is a relaxed group discussion at the end of class — no marks, just honest sharing
Theatre teaches you empathy — understanding how others feel, like a true hero would
Collaboration in theatre, just like in a team sport, makes everyone stronger together

summary: In this exciting quest into the realm of Theatre, you discovered that drama is a powerful wizard that teaches emotions, teamwork, and life skills — not just acting! You played the Hot Seat game to unlock your emotions and learnt that feelings can mix like Holi colours. The ancient rishis of India gave us the incredible gift of Navarasa — nine emotion-dragons that cover every feeling a human can have, shaped by your own inner bhava or attitude. You also met Bharata Muni, the great wizard who wrote the Natyashastra, a 2000-year-old spell book on all performing arts, called the Fifth Veda. Theatre is your quest — a safe, joyful, creative space where your voice, face, and body all work together, and every brave idea you share makes you stronger!
